componentDescription: SingleSelect is the standard dropdown for selecting one value from a list. Use it for form fields like country, category, status, or any enumerated single-choice. For multi-choice, use MultiSelect. For simple navigation menus, use Menu. SingleSelect supports search, virtualization for large lists, sub-menus, and mobile drawer mode.
features: Two variants: CONTAINER (bordered trigger) and NO_CONTAINER (minimal trigger)
Grouped items with optional group labels and separators
Virtualized rendering for large item lists
Infinite scroll with onEndReached and hasMore
compositionPattern: Items are passed as SelectMenuGroupType[]. Each group has an optional groupLabel and an items array of SelectMenuItemType. If you don't need groups, pass a single group without a groupLabel. Each item needs at minimum label and value strings.

usageExamples.title: Basic country selector
usageExamples.description: Single select for country with search enabled.
usageExamples.code:
<SingleSelect
  label="Country"
  placeholder="Select country"
  selected={selectedCountry}
  onSelect={setSelectedCountry}
  enableSearch
  searchPlaceholder="Search countries..."
  size={SelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  items={[
    {
      items: countries.map(c => ({ label: c.name, value: c.code }))
    }
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Status selector with grouped items
usageExamples.description: Grouped dropdown for ticket status with active and archived categories.
usageExamples.code:
<SingleSelect
  label="Status"
  placeholder="Select status"
  selected={status}
  onSelect={setStatus}
  size={SelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  variant={SelectMenuVariant.CONTAINER}
  items={[
    {
      groupLabel: 'Active',
      items: [
        { label: 'Open', value: 'open', slot1: <GreenDot /> },
        { label: 'In Progress', value: 'in_progress', slot1: <YellowDot /> },
      ]
    },
    {
      groupLabel: 'Closed',
      showSeparator: true,
      items: [
        { label: 'Resolved', value: 'resolved', slot1: <BlueDot /> },
        { label: 'Cancelled', value: 'cancelled', slot1: <GreyDot /> },
      ]
    }
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Large list with virtualization and infinite scroll
usageExamples.description: Virtualized select for a large user list with infinite scroll.
usageExamples.code:
<SingleSelect
  label="Assign to"
  placeholder="Select team member"
  selected={assignee}
  onSelect={setAssignee}
  enableSearch
  enableVirtualization
  hasMore={hasMoreUsers}
  onEndReached={loadMoreUsers}
  loadingComponent={<Spinner />}
  size={SelectMenuSize.MEDIUM}
  items={[{ items: users.map(u => ({ label: u.name, value: u.id, slot1: <Avatar name={u.name} /> })) }]}
/>

placeholder.llmContext: Required. Text shown when no option is selected. Use descriptive action phrases like 'Select a country' or 'Choose status' rather than generic 'Select...'.
placeholder.propDescription: Required. Trigger text when no item is selected

items.llmContext: Required. Array of SelectMenuGroupType. Each group: { groupLabel?: string, items: SelectMenuItemType[], showSeparator?: boolean }. Each item: { label: string, value: string, subLabel?: string, slot1?: ReactNode, slot2?: ReactNode, slot3?: ReactNode, disabled?: boolean, onClick?: () => void, subMenu?: SelectMenuGroupType[], tooltip?: string }. For non-grouped lists, pass a single group object with no groupLabel.
items.propDescription: Required. Grouped array of selectable items

selected.llmContext: Required. The currently selected value string (matching a value in your items array). Pass empty string or null/undefined for no selection. Always manage with useState.

onSelect.llmContext: Required. Callback fired with the selected item's value string when user picks an option. Update your selected state here.
onSelect.propDescription: Required. Called with selected value string on item pick

enableSearch.llmContext: Set to true when the item list has more than 7-8 items. Adds a search input at the top of the dropdown. Essential for country lists, user lists, and any long enumeration.
enableSearch.propDescription: Adds a search input to filter items

variant.llmContext: CONTAINER (default) renders a bordered input-like trigger — use in forms. NO_CONTAINER renders a minimal trigger with no border — use for settings toggles, inline editors, or toolbar selectors.

useDrawerOnMobile.llmContext: Set to true for dropdowns used in mobile-responsive UIs. Automatically switches to a full bottom-sheet Drawer on small screens, which is much easier to interact with on touch devices.
useDrawerOnMobile.propDescription: Renders as bottom-sheet Drawer on mobile

enableVirtualization.llmContext: Set to true when the items list exceeds ~100 items. Renders only visible items in the DOM, preventing performance issues. Required for user lists, product catalogs, and other large datasets.
enableVirtualization.propDescription: Enables virtual rendering for large item lists

allowDeselect.llmContext: Set to true to allow the user to click a selected item again to deselect it (clearing the value). Use for optional fields where 'no selection' is a valid state.
allowDeselect.propDescription: Allows clicking selected item to deselect it

allowCustomValue.llmContext: Set to true to let users type a custom value not in the items list. Useful for open-ended fields like tags or categories where the full list of valid values isn't known in advance.
allowCustomValue.propDescription: Allows user to enter a custom value not in the items list

inline.llmContext: Set to true to render the dropdown inline in the document flow instead of as a portal. Use in scrollable containers or when z-index conflicts make portals problematic.
inline.propDescription: Renders dropdown inline instead of as a portal
